mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower equity discount rates and improve corporate financing conditions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based collapse in consumer demand and wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with accuracy below 45%, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation above 210.0 forces monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing equity valuation multiples compression and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of high-inflation periods, or inflation shows no negative correlation with equity returns in rolling window analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the industrial production index reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boost corporate profit expectations and yield positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 1-year rolling windows or 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with INDPRO changes.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly GDP growth confirms economic contraction, triggering recession risk premiums and portfolio de-risking that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters following negative GDP prints, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear markets in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates signals structural labor market deterioration and wage deflation risks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal predicts positive returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1100.0 thousand units indicate a severe real estate downturn that amplifies financial sector losses and consumer deleveraging, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of periods when HOUST drops below 1100.0, or the housing signal loses predictive power after controlling for interest rate changes.
